impact: The blocked checkbox-27 item is moved out of selectable checkbox form, preserving accepted work while allowing checkbox-29 and later fixture-first tasks to proceed. The repair guide now explicitly tells the daemon to stop retrying malformed mutation-validator prompts after repeated py_compile failures, run an exact py_compile preflight first, and resume independent tasks instead of spinning on the same syntax failure.
summary: Park repeated checkbox-27 syntax retries and tighten supervisor syntax-recovery guidance so the daemon can continue with independent validation-first work.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 1 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-27: Recovery for checkbox-27: add the one-file mutation validator for `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/permit_lifecycle_requirement_extraction_expected_output.json` using only syntactically simple Python, standard-library `unittest`, exact fixture loading, an initial committed-fixture-valid assertion, one mutation per test, and an explicit `py_compile`-first self-check. Cover missing `categoryId`, missing `sourceEvidenceIds`, missing local `evidence[].evidenceId`, out-of-range `confidence`, duplicate `(categoryId, action, object, sourceEvidenceIds)` tuples, and malformed stop-gate classification. Do not add extractor implementation code.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
